title: Voices | Investing in the future: ensuring women-led small businesses can thrive
gist: World Bank Group and Visa Foundation announce a partnership (starting in India, South Africa, Nigeria, Colombia, Mexico) to close the trillion-dollar financing gap for women-led small businesses via tailored finance, skills, and digital innovation. The most concrete signal is an India pilot using credit-enhanced 'gender bonds' to fund the missing-middle transition between microfinance and commercial credit.

insights.claim: Small and medium businesses are around 90 percent of all businesses worldwide and account for more than half of global employment, making them — not multinationals — the primary engine of jobs in emerging economies.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Anchors development job-creation strategy on SMEs rather than large-firm FDI, reshaping where catalytic capital should flow.
insights.quote: Small and medium-sized businesses represent around 90 percent of all businesses worldwide, and account for more than half of global employment.
insights.locator: Small and medium-sized businesses represent around 90 percent

insights.claim: The global financing gap for women-led businesses remains in the trillions of dollars, with women excluded from traditional financial channels even when they can access capital they lack relevant business training.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Sizes an under-served credit market that fintech and development finance are positioning to capture.
insights.quote: The financing gap for women-led businesses globally remains in the trillions of dollars.
insights.locator: Yet many small businesses in developing countries remain cash deprived

insights.claim: New alternative data sources beyond conventional credit histories, digital distribution channels, and AI-assisted credit-decision and training tools are what make serving women-led businesses viable at scale.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Points to AI and alternative-data underwriting as the technology enabling profitable lending to thin-file borrowers in EMs.
insights.quote: AI-assisted tools for credit decisions and training are making it possible for financial institutions to serve women-led businesses more effectively and at scale
insights.locator: What changes the equation is innovation.

insights.claim: The World Bank Group and Visa Foundation formed a partnership focused first on India, South Africa, Nigeria, Colombia, and Mexico to deliver tailored finance and training to women entrepreneurs.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: A named public-private partnership with target markets indicates where women's-finance capital and pilots will concentrate.
insights.quote: That's why the World Bank Group and Visa Foundation have teamed up.
insights.locator: Harnessing these innovations calls for the public and private sectors

themes.theme: Gender bonds and the missing middle

insights.claim: An India World Bank Group pilot tests credit-enhanced 'gender bonds' to fund the hard transition where a woman-led business has outgrown self-help-group and microfinance borrowing but is not yet eligible for mainstream commercial credit.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Identifies a specific, investable instrument (gender bonds) and the 'missing middle' market segment it targets.
insights.quote: the World Bank Group works with large development finance institutions and a vast network of non-bank financial companies to test credit-enhanced "gender bonds."
insights.locator: For example, in India, a World Bank Group project

insights.claim: Gender bonds act as a cheaper route for non-bank lenders to raise long-term funding, connecting them to investors they could not otherwise reach and enabling longer-tenure, more affordable loans for women entrepreneurs.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Mechanism explains how a capital-markets instrument lowers the cost of funding for women's-finance lenders.
insights.quote: These bonds act as a cheaper route for these lenders to raise long-term funding, helping connect them to investors they couldn't otherwise reach.
insights.locator: The solution aims to unlock longer tenures

insights.claim: In Latin America, Visa Foundation partnered with Pro Mujer to launch Emprende Pro Mujer, a digital on-demand skills platform for women entrepreneurs at every business stage.
insights.speaker: Najada Kumbuli, Sophie Sirtaine
insights.so_what: Shows the skills-side complement to capital, signaling a bundled finance-plus-training model.
insights.quote: Visa Foundation partnered with Pro Mujer to launch Emprende Pro Mujer, a digital, on-demand platform for women entrepreneurs
insights.locator: In Latin America, Visa Foundation partnered with Pro Mujer
